13	/-!
14	# The augmented Čech complex is exact (blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`)
15	
16	This file proves that the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is exact
17	(every homology object is the zero object in `X.Modules`) whenever `𝒰` is a finite
18	affine open cover of `X` with all pairwise intersections affine (ensured by separatedness
19	of `X`) and `F` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module.
20	
21	The theorem is placed here rather than in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` to avoid an import
22	cycle: every ingredient of the sections/sheafification proof route
23	(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`,
24	`affineCoverSystem`, `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`) lives in a file that transitively imports
25	`CechHigherDirectImage.lean`.  Placing `cechAugmented_exact` in this downstream file
26	(`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`) makes all those ingredients available without a cycle.
27	The pure-Mathlib site lemmas (`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W`,
28	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`, `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`)
29	live in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` and are importable here.
30	
31	Blueprint: `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
32	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
33	  `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.
34	-/
35	
36	universe u
37	
38	open CategoryTheory Limits
39	
40	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	open Scheme.Modules
43	
44	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
45	
46	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/
47	
48	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
49	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then
50	`X` is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` +
51	`Functor.map_injective`; lighter than `reflects_exact_of_faithful`, which needs
52	`[Abelian]`.) Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory). -/
53	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
54	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [HasZeroMorphisms C] [HasZeroMorphisms D]
55	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
56	    (h : IsZero (F.obj Y)) : IsZero Y := by
57	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
58	  apply F.map_injective
59	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
60	  exact h
61	
62	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — a null-homotopic identity kills homology -/
63	
64	/-- **A complex homotopy-equivalent to zero has vanishing homology.**
65	If the identity of a homological complex `D` is homotopic to the zero map
66	(`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`), then every homology object `D.homology p` is a zero object.
67	This is the categorical packaging of the contracting-homotopy criterion: the
68	prepend-`i_fix` homotopy on the section {\v C}ech complex produces exactly such a
69	`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`, and this lemma converts it into the required
70	`IsZero (D.homology p)`.
71	
72	The proof is the standard three-lemma combo: homotopy-invariance of the induced
73	homology map (`Homotopy.homologyMap_eq`), the identity/zero computations
74	(`homologyMap_id`, `homologyMap_zero`), and the "identity equals zero" criterion for a
75	zero object (`IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero`).  Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological
76	algebra); blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`, formalization mechanism Step 3(d). -/
77	lemma isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
78	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (D : HomologicalComplex C c) (p : ι)
79	    [D.HasHomology p] (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) := by
80	  refine (IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero _).mpr ?_
81	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_id, ho.homologyMap_eq p, HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_zero]
82	
83	/-- **Transport `IsZero` of homology across a complex iso plus a null-homotopic identity.**
84	If `D ≅ D'` as homological complexes and the identity of `D'` is homotopic to `0`, then
85	`D.homology p` is a zero object.  This is the consumer-side glue for the section-{\v C}ech
86	route: the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech complex `D` is identified with a concrete section
87	complex `D'` (`cechSection_complex_iso`) which carries a contracting homotopy
88	(`cechSection_contractible`); this lemma turns that pair into `IsZero (D.homology p)`.
89	Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological algebra). -/
90	lemma isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
91	    [CategoryWithHomology C] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι}
92	    {D D' : HomologicalComplex C c} (e : D ≅ D') (p : ι)
93	    (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) :=
94	  IsZero.of_iso (isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero D' p ho)
95	    ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor C c p).mapIso e)
96	
97	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a locally-zero presheaf -/
98	
99	open Opposite in
100	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf that is locally a zero object vanishes.**
101	If for every object `U` of the site there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U` all of whose
102	members `g : V ⟶ U` land on an object `V` where `Q.obj (op V)` is a zero object, then
103	the sheafification of `Q` is the zero object.
104	
105	This packages the three imported site lemmas
106	(`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`) into the "locally zero" form the
107	{\v C}ech-resolution argument consumes: the presheaf homology `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V, F)` is a zero
108	object over every `V` contained in a cover member, and those `V` are a covering basis.
109	The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero presheaf) comes from the
110	hypothesis (each member's section group is a subsingleton); the local surjectivity is
111	free (the target is a zero object). Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
112	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C]
113	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
114	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
115	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
116	    (h : ∀ U : C, ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
117	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → IsZero (Q.obj (op V))) :
118	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by
119	  let Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
120	  have hZobj : ∀ x : Cᵒᵖ, IsZero (Z.obj x) := by
121	    intro x
122	    change IsZero (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
123	    exact AddCommGrpCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
124	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
125	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
126	    refine ⟨?_⟩
127	    intro X x y _
128	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop
129	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
130	    intro V g hg
131	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y
132	    haveI := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSzero hg)
133	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
134	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
135	    refine ⟨?_⟩
136	    intro U s
137	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
138	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
139	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
140	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
141	  exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective
142	    J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ

144	/-- **The evaluated augmented Čech section complex has vanishing homology over any open
145	contained in a cover member** (blueprint `lem:cechSection_isZero_homology`).
146	
147	For `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`, the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F`, pushed
148	through the forgetful/restrict-scalars adapter and evaluated at `V`, has zero homology in
149	every degree.  This is the consumer-facing packaging of the Sub-brick A chain in
150	`CechSectionIdentification.lean`: the evaluated complex is identified with the concrete
151	augmented section Čech complex (`cechSection_complex_iso`), which carries the
152	prepend-`i` contracting homotopy (`cechSection_contractible`, valid since
153	`V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`); the in-file glue `isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero`
154	combines the pair. -/
155	lemma cechSection_isZero_homology (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
156	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) (p : ℕ) :
157	    let α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj
158	    let cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ
159	    let K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F
160	    let Kp := ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
161	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K
162	    let GV :=
163	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
164	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)
165	    IsZero (((GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj Kp).homology p) :=
166	  isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero (cechSection_complex_iso 𝒰 F V) p
167	    (cechSection_contractible 𝒰 F V i hiV)

169	/- Planner strategy:
170	Route: sections + sheafification (NOT stalks — `SheafOfModules.stalk` is absent from Mathlib).
171	Step 1: reflect `IsZero (homology p)` through the faithful additive forgetful functor
172	`SheafOfModules.toSheaf` (it preserves zero morphisms, so it reflects the zero object). Step 2:
173	the homology SHEAF = sheafification of the PRESHEAF homology, via the project engine
174	`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` (HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean). Step 3: the presheaf
175	homology is `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V,F)`, locally zero on the affine basis — over each basic affine `D(g) ⊆ Uᵢ`,
176	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` gives `F|_{D(g)} ≅ ~M` and
177	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` (CechAcyclic.lean) kills positive-degree homology;
178	the basic affines are cofinal (`standard_cover_cofinal` / `affineCoverSystem`), so the map
179	`0 → presheaf-homology` is locally bijective, hence its sheafification (= the homology sheaf) is
180	zero. The reusable abelian-sheaf site lemmas `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W` /`_of_W_isZero`/
181	`_of_isLocallyBijective` (in CechHigherDirectImage.lean, importable) discharge the site-theory half.
182	The ONE bridge to build here: connect the module-level `homologyIsoSheafify` to those abelian-sheaf
183	site lemmas via the sheafification square `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ forget`
184	(cf. `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf`, used in AffineSerreVanishing.lean; see
185	`analogies/tosheaf-epi.md` and the iter-053 mathlib-analogist report on the toSheaf-reflection
186	bridge). Diamond-prone — work with `.hom` not `.val`, defeq/`change` not `rw`. Step 4: the
187	degree-0 augmentation node uses the same spanning-family exactness (`exact_of_isLocalized_span` /
188	`combDifferential_exact`). Blueprint: `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
189	-/
190	
191	/-- **The augmented Čech complex is a resolution** (Stacks
192	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`; blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`).
193	
194	Let `X` be a separated scheme, `𝒰` a finite open cover of `X` all of whose members are
195	affine (so every finite intersection `U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{iₚ}` is affine by separatedness),
196	and `F` a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. Then every homology object of the augmented Čech
197	complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is zero:
198	```
199	  ∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p).
200	```
201	Equivalently, the {\v C}ech nerve of `𝒰` is a resolution of `F` in `X.Modules`. -/
202	theorem cechAugmented_exact (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
203	    (h𝒰 : ∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)) [X.IsSeparated]
204	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
205	    ∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p) := by
206	  intro p
207	  -- Step 1: reflect through the faithful, zero-preserving `toSheaf`.
208	  apply isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
209	  -- Step 2: transport `IsZero` across `homologyIsoSheafify` and the sheafification square.
210	  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
211	  set cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ with hcc
212	  set K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F with hK
213	  set P := ((((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
214	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K).homology p) with hP
215	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
216	    ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
217	        (PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify α cc K p) ≪≫
218	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
219	  -- Step 3: the presheaf homology is locally a zero object on the cover-member basis.
220	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
221	  apply isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Q := Q)
222	  -- Section-level vanishing on every open `V` contained in a cover member `Uᵢ`:
223	  -- the restricted cover `{Uₛ ∩ V}` has the member `Uᵢ ∩ V = V`, giving a contracting
224	  -- (prepend-`i_fix`) homotopy on the augmented section complex over `V`. (F-agnostic.)
225	  have hSec : ∀ (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i : 𝒰.I₀),
226	      V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i → IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
227	    intro V i hiV
228	    -- Evaluate-at-`V` the underlying presheaf complex: this functor is additive and
229	    -- preserves homology, so `Q.obj (op V) ≅ Hᵖ(Γ(V, K•))` (the section complex homology).
230	    set GV := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
231	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V) with hGV
232	    set Kp := (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
233	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K) with hKp
234	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GV.mapHomologyIso' cc Kp p).symm
235	    -- Remaining: the augmented section complex `Γ(V, cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F)` is exact
236	    -- in degree `p`.  Since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`, the restricted cover `{Uₛ ∩ V}` has the
237	    -- member `coverOpen 𝒰 i ∩ V = V`; prepending that fixed index `i` is a contracting
238	    -- homotopy `d∘h + h∘d = id` on the section complex (template
239	    -- `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` / the objectwise homotopy of FreePresheafComplex),
240	    -- so every homology object vanishes.  F-agnostic, cover-agnostic.
241	    -- Step 3(d): discharged by `cechSection_isZero_homology` above (the Sub-brick A chain
242	    -- of `CechSectionIdentification.lean`, packaged via `isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero`).
243	    exact cechSection_isZero_homology 𝒰 F V i hiV p
244	  -- Assemble the covering sieve `{g : V ⟶ U | V ≤ some Uᵢ}` of every open `U`.
245	  intro U
246	  refine ⟨{ arrows := fun {V} _ => ∃ i, V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i,
247	            downward_closed := fun {V W} g hg h => ?_ }, ?_, ?_⟩
248	  · obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hg
249	    exact ⟨i, le_trans (leOfHom h) hi⟩
250	  · -- membership in the opens topology: cover `U` pointwise via `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ = ⊤`.
251	    intro x hx
252	    have hx' : x ∈ (⨆ i, coverOpen 𝒰 i) := by
253	      have htop : (⨆ i, coverOpen 𝒰 i) = ⊤ := Scheme.OpenCover.iSup_opensRange 𝒰
254	      rw [htop]; trivial
255	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp hx'
256	    exact ⟨U ⊓ coverOpen 𝒰 i, homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, inf_le_right⟩, hx, hi⟩
257	  · rintro V g ⟨i, hi⟩
258	    exact hSec V i hi

260	end AlgebraicGeometry

31	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/

33	namespace CategoryTheory.Functor
34	
35	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
36	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then `X`
37	is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` + `Functor.map_injective`.)
38	
39	Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory).  A copy of the same lemma in
40	`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`; reproduced here because that sibling file is not in this
41	file's import chain. -/
42	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
43	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms D]
44	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
45	    (h : Limits.IsZero (F.obj Y)) : Limits.IsZero Y := by
46	  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
47	  apply F.map_injective
48	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
49	  exact h
50	
51	end CategoryTheory.Functor

53	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a sectionwise-locally-zero presheaf -/
54	
55	namespace CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology
56	
57	open Opposite in
58	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf whose sections are locally zero vanishes.**
59	If for every object `U` and every section `s : Q(U)` there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U`
60	on which `s` restricts to `0`, then the sheafification of `Q` is a zero object.
61	
62	This is the *sectionwise* (local-injectivity) strengthening of
63	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero`: that lemma requires the *objectwise* vanishing
64	`Q.obj (op V) = 0` for every member `V` of a single covering sieve, which forces the
65	vanishing to hold on a downward-closed family.  Here only the restricted *section* need
66	vanish, so the witnessing sieve can be generated by a cofinal *basis* of "good" opens
67	(e.g. the affine opens, where the section group vanishes) even though the basis itself is
68	not downward closed.  The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero
69	presheaf) is read off from the hypothesis applied to `x - y`; local surjectivity is free.
70	Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
71	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero {C : Type*} [Category C]
72	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
73	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
74	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
75	    (h : ∀ (U : C) (s : ToType (Q.obj (op U))), ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
76	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) s = 0) :
77	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by
78	  let Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
79	  have hZobj : ∀ x : Cᵒᵖ, IsZero (Z.obj x) := by
80	    intro x
81	    change IsZero (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
82	    exact AddCommGrpCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
83	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
84	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
85	    refine ⟨?_⟩
86	    intro X x y _
87	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop (x - y)
88	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
89	    intro V g hg
90	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y
91	    have h0 : (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) (x - y) = 0 := hSzero hg
92	    rw [map_sub] at h0
93	    exact sub_eq_zero.mp h0
94	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
95	    refine ⟨?_⟩
96	    intro U s
97	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
98	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
99	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
100	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
101	  exact isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ
102	
103	end CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology

115	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — corepresentability of the sections functor
116	
117	The sections functor `Γ(V, -)` on `X.Modules` is corepresented by the Form-B object
118	`jShriekOU V`.  Consequently its right derived functors agree, degreewise, with those of the
119	Hom-functor `Hom(jShriekOU V, -)` — the homological half of the identification
120	`Rᵠ Γ(V, -) ≅ Ext^q(jShriekOU V, -)` (the deep "Bridge (1)/(2)" connecting the higher-direct-image
121	presheaf homology to absolute cohomology). -/

123	/-- **Naturality of `jShriekOU_homEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf** (reproduced here; the version
124	in `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` is `private`). Composing a map `jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁` with `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂`
125	corresponds, under corepresentability, to applying `g` on sections over `V`. -/
126	private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
127	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
128	    jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
129	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (op V)))
130	          (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁ h) := by
131	  have hs : sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
132	        = sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
133	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
134	        (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
135	  change freeYonedaHomEquiv V _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
136	  rw [hs, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.comp_apply]
137	  rfl

139	/-- `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X` is additive (it is the additive composite
140	`forget ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙)`). Project-local instance so `sectionsFunctor` is additive. -/
141	noncomputable instance toPresheafOfModules_additive :
142	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).Additive :=
143	  inferInstanceAs (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
144	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive

146	/-- The sections functor `Γ(V,-) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab` is additive. Project-local instance (the flat
147	triple composite does not infer additivity automatically). -/
148	noncomputable instance sectionsFunctor_additive (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
149	    (AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).Additive := by
150	  unfold AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor
151	  haveI i2 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
152	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (op V)).Additive :=
153	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
154	  exact Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _

156	/-- **The sections functor `Γ(V,-)` is corepresented by `jShriekOU V`.** A natural isomorphism
157	`sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V))`, assembled from the additive
158	corepresentability bijection `jShriekOU_homEquiv` and its naturality. Project-local: the functorial
159	upgrade of `jShriekOU_homEquiv`, the geometric input to the absolute-cohomology bridge. -/
160	noncomputable def sectionsFunctorCorepIso (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
161	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V)) :=
162	  NatIso.ofComponents
163	    (fun F => (AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F)).symm)
164	    (by
165	      intro F₁ F₂ g
166	      ext s
167	      simp only [AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso, comp_apply, Iso.symm_hom, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
168	        AddEquiv.coe_toAddMonoidHom]
169	      rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_eq]
170	      change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).map g)) s
171	        = jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s ≫ g)
172	      rw [jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s) g, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
173	      rfl)

175	/-- **Right derived functors transport along a natural isomorphism of additive functors.**
176	Project-local: assembled from `NatTrans.rightDerived` of `τ.hom`/`τ.inv` and functoriality
177	(`NatTrans.rightDerived_comp`, `NatTrans.rightDerived_id`). -/
178	noncomputable def rightDerivedNatIso {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Abelian C]
179	    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [Abelian D] {F G : C ⥤ D} [F.Additive] [G.Additive]
180	    (τ : F ≅ G) (n : ℕ) : F.rightDerived n ≅ G.rightDerived n where
181	  hom := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.hom n
182	  inv := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.inv n
183	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.hom_inv_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]
184	  inv_hom_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.inv_hom_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]

186	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext` transport along the spectrum equivalence (Need #1)
187	
188	The whole-scheme spectrum isomorphism `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`, a genuine iso because
189	`U` is affine) induces an *equivalence* of module categories `U.Modules ≌ (Spec Γ U).Modules`,
190	assembled from `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` along the iso and its coherences. An equivalence functor
191	is fully faithful, exact, and preserves injective objects, so
192	`Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects` upgrades `Ext.mapExactFunctor` along it
193	to an *isomorphism* of `Ext`-groups. This transports the residual general-affine-open Serre
194	vanishing for `U` to the `Spec`-developed `affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`.
195	
196	The pushforward-of-an-iso construction is generic in the scheme isomorphism `φ : X ≅ Y`. -/
197	
198	/-- **A scheme isomorphism induces an equivalence of module categories.** For `φ : X ≅ Y`, the
199	pushforward `Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom` is an equivalence with quasi-inverse the pushforward
200	along `φ.inv`; the unit/counit are supplied by the
201	`pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` coherences. Project-local: Mathlib packages the
202	pushforward functor and its coherences but not the equivalence assembled from an iso (the genuine
203	equivalence used to transport `Ext` for Need #1). -/
204	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
205	    X.Modules ≌ Y.Modules :=
206	  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk
207	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom) (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.inv)
208	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).symm ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.hom_inv_id.symm ≪≫
209	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.hom φ.inv).symm)
210	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.inv φ.hom ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.inv_hom_id ≪≫
211	      Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y)
212	
213	/-- The functor of `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` is (defeq) the additive `pushforward φ.hom`. -/
214	noncomputable instance pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
215	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.Additive :=
216	  inferInstanceAs (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom).Additive

249	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext`-vanishing ⟹ coyoneda right-derived vanishing
250	
251	The homological-algebra half of the relative-affine vanishing argument (Bridge (1)/(2) remainder).
252	The `q`-th right derived functor of the additive Hom-functor `Hom(P,-) = preadditiveCoyoneda(op P)`,
253	evaluated at `H`, is computed (via the chosen injective resolution `I•` of `H`,
254	`InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`) as the homology at degree `q` of the cochain complex of
255	abelian groups `n ↦ (P ⟶ Iⁿ)`.  By `InjectiveResolution.extMk` / `extMk_eq_zero_iff`, that homology
256	*is* `Ext P H q`: a degree-`q` cocycle is a map `f : P ⟶ Iq` with `f ≫ d = 0`, and it is a coboundary
257	iff the class `extMk f` vanishes in `Ext`.  Hence if every class of `Ext P H q` vanishes (`q ≥ 1`),
258	the Hom-complex is exact at `q` and the right-derived object is a zero object.  Project-local: Mathlib's
259	`Ext` is built on the derived category, not on `Functor.rightDerived`, so this bridge is absent. -/
260	
261	/-- The differential of the Hom-cochain-complex `n ↦ (P ⟶ Iⁿ)` (the image of a cochain complex `K`
262	under `preadditiveCoyoneda(op P)`) acts by post-composition with the differential of `K`. -/
263	private lemma preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply {C : Type*} [Category C]
264	    [Preadditive C] (P : C) (K : CochainComplex C ℕ) (i j : ℕ) (g : P ⟶ K.X i) :
265	    (((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).d i j g
266	      = g ≫ K.d i j := by
267	  rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d]
268	  rfl
269	
270	/-- **`Ext`-vanishing forces the coyoneda right-derived object to vanish.** If every class of
271	`Ext P H q` is zero (`q ≥ 1`), then `(Hom(P,-)).rightDerived q` is a zero object at `H`. The
272	homological-algebra bridge between the project's `Ext`-based Serre vanishing and the
273	`Functor.rightDerived` description of the higher-direct-image presheaf homology. Project-local. -/
274	lemma isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero {C : Type*} [Category C]
275	    [Abelian C] [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [CategoryTheory.HasExt C] (P H : C) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q)
276	    (hExt : ∀ e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext P H q, e = 0) :
277	    IsZero (((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)).rightDerived q).obj H) := by
278	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hq.ne'
279	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
280	    (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)) (p + 1))
281	  set R := injectiveResolution H with hR
282	  refine HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology ?_
283	  rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff' _ p (p + 1) (p + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
284	    ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff]
285	  intro x₂ hx₂
286	  have hf : x₂ ≫ R.cocomplex.d (p + 1) (p + 2) = 0 := by
287	    rw [← preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply P R.cocomplex (p + 1) (p + 2) x₂]
288	    exact hx₂
289	  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := (R.extMk_eq_zero_iff x₂ (p + 2) rfl hf p rfl).mp (hExt _)
290	  refine ⟨g, ?_⟩
291	  rw [← hg]
292	  exact preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply P R.cocomplex p (p + 1) g
293	
294	/-- **`[HasInjectiveResolutions C]` provides enough injectives.** The degree-`0` term of the chosen
295	injective resolution, with its quasi-iso unit, is an injective presentation. Project-local connector
296	(Mathlib derives `HasInjectiveResolutions` from `EnoughInjectives` + `Abelian`, not conversely);
297	needed to feed `[EnoughInjectives U.Modules]` to the spectrum `Ext`-transport from
298	`[HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]`. -/
299	lemma enoughInjectives_of_hasInjectiveResolutions {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
300	    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] : EnoughInjectives C where
301	  presentation X := by
302	    obtain ⟨R⟩ := (HasInjectiveResolutions.out X).out
303	    exact ⟨{ J := R.cocomplex.X 0, injective := R.injective 0, f := R.ι.f 0
304	             mono := CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι R 0 }⟩

306	/-- **An isomorphism in the first `Ext`-argument transfers subsingletons.** If `Ext B Y q` is a
307	subsingleton and `φ : A ≅ B`, then so is `Ext A Y q` (every class is the `φ.hom`-precomposite of its
308	`φ.inv`-precomposite, which vanishes). Project-local: the contravariant `Ext`-functoriality packaged
309	for the Serre-vanishing transport along the `jShriekOU` transport iso. -/
310	lemma subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [CategoryTheory.HasExt C]
311	    {A B : C} (φ : A ≅ B) (Y : C) (q : ℕ)
312	    [Subsingleton (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext B Y q)] :
313	    Subsingleton (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A Y q) := by
314	  refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
315	  suffices h : ∀ z : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A Y q, z = 0 by rw [h a, h b]
316	  intro z
317	  have hw : (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.inv).comp z (zero_add q) = 0 := Subsingleton.elim _ _
318	  have hz : z = (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.hom).comp
319	      ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.inv).comp z (zero_add q)) (zero_add q) := by
320	    rw [Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀_assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
321	  rw [hz, hw, Abelian.Ext.comp_zero]

323	/-- **Serre `Ext`-vanishing over an affine scheme via the spectrum transport.** Let `U` be a scheme,
324	`φ : U ≅ Spec R` a scheme isomorphism, `V ⊆ U` an open and `H` an `𝒪_U`-module.  If the pushforward
325	`Φ(j_!𝒪_V)` of the corepresenting object is isomorphic to `j_!𝒪_{V'}` for an *affine* open
326	`V' ⊆ Spec R`, and `Φ H` is quasi-coherent, then every class of `Ext^q_U(j_!𝒪_V, H)` vanishes for
327	`q ≥ 1`.  Assembled from `pushforwardExtAddEquiv` (the spectrum transport), the contravariant
328	`Ext`-transfer `subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst`, and the now-unconditional
329	`affine_serre_vanishing_general_open` on `Spec R`.  Project-local: the Bridge (2) Serre-vanishing
330	leaf of the open-immersion acyclicity, parameterized over the geometric transport data so that the
331	residual `jShriekOU`/quasi-coherence transport sub-lemmas are isolated. -/
332	lemma ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (U : Scheme.{u})
333	    [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules] (φ : U ≅ Spec R) (V : U.Opens) (H : U.Modules)
334	    (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (V' : (Spec R).Opens) (hV' : IsAffineOpen V')
335	    (hjt : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU V')
336	    (hqc : ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent)
337	    (e : Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) H q) : e = 0 := by
338	  haveI : EnoughInjectives U.Modules := enoughInjectives_of_hasInjectiveResolutions
339	  set Φ := Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ with hΦ
340	  haveI : EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules := EnoughInjectives.of_equivalence Φ.inverse
341	  haveI : (Φ.functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := hqc
342	  haveI hsub0 : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V') (Φ.functor.obj H) q) :=
343	    ⟨fun a b => (affine_serre_vanishing_general_open _ V' hV' q hq a).trans
344	      (affine_serre_vanishing_general_open _ V' hV' q hq b).symm⟩
345	  haveI hsub1 : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext (Φ.functor.obj (jShriekOU V)) (Φ.functor.obj H) q) :=
346	    subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst hjt (Φ.functor.obj H) q
347	  have htr := Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv φ (jShriekOU V) H q
348	  exact htr.injective (Subsingleton.elim (htr e) (htr 0))

350	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `jShriekOU` transport along a scheme iso (Need #1)
351	
352	The corepresenting object `jShriekOU V` of `Γ(V,-)` is carried along a scheme isomorphism
353	`φ : X ≅ Y` by the induced pushforward equivalence: `Φ.functor (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.
354	The argument is pure corepresentability transport — `jShriekOU V` is never unfolded.  Both sides
355	corepresent the *same* `Type`-valued sections functor `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`: the right side directly (the
356	additive corepresentability `jShriekOU_homEquiv`), the left side via the pushforward adjunction
357	(`Φ.functor ⊣ Φ.inverse`) composed with the definitional identity "sections of a pushforward =
358	sections over the preimage open".  `CorepresentableBy.uniqueUpToIso` then produces the iso. -/
359	
360	/-- **`jShriekOU V` corepresents the `Type`-valued sections functor `Γ(V,-)`.**  Repackages the
361	additive corepresentability `jShriekOU_homEquiv` as a `CorepresentableBy` of `sectionsFunctor V`
362	post-composed with the forgetful functor to types. Project-local. -/
363	private noncomputable def sectionsCorep {X : Scheme.{u}} (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
364	    (sectionsFunctor V ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat).CorepresentableBy (jShriekOU V) where
365	  homEquiv {B} := (jShriekOU_homEquiv V B).toEquiv
366	  homEquiv_comp g f := jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V f g
367	
368	/-- **The pushforward of `jShriekOU V` corepresents `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`.**  Via the pushforward
369	adjunction `Φ.functor ⊣ Φ.inverse` and the definitional identity "sections of `φ.inv`-pushforward
370	over `V` = sections over `φ.inv⁻¹V`", the object `Φ.functor (jShriekOU V)` corepresents the same
371	`Type`-valued sections functor as `jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.  Project-local. -/
372	private noncomputable def sectionsCorepPushforward {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
373	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
374	    (sectionsFunctor (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V) ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat).CorepresentableBy
375	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V)) where
376	  homEquiv {B} :=
377	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).toAdjunction.homEquiv (jShriekOU V) B).trans
378	      (jShriekOU_homEquiv V ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).inverse.obj B)).toEquiv
379	  homEquiv_comp {B B'} g f := by
380	    simp only [Equiv.trans_apply]
381	    rw [(Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).toAdjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
382	    erw [jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V
383	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).toAdjunction.homEquiv (jShriekOU V) B f)
384	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).inverse.map g)]
385	
386	/-- **A scheme isomorphism carries `jShriekOU` along** (blueprint `lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso`).
387	For `φ : X ≅ Y` and an open `V ⊆ X`, the pushforward equivalence sends the corepresenting object of
388	`Γ(V,-)` to that of `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`:
389	`Φ.functor (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.  Project-local: the geometric naturality of `j_!`
390	under a scheme iso (the first-`Ext`-argument transport of Need #1). -/
391	noncomputable def jShriekOU_transport_along_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
392	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
393	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅
394	      jShriekOU (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V) :=
395	  (sectionsCorepPushforward φ V).uniqueUpToIso (sectionsCorep (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V))

397	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherence transport along a scheme iso (`hqc`) -/
398	
399	/-- **A scheme isomorphism transports an open cover.** If a family `Z` of opens covers `X`
400	(in the sense of the opens Grothendieck topology, i.e. `CoversTop`), then the preimage family
401	`φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ (Z i)` covers `Y`.  Project-local: the cover-transport half of
402	`pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh` (Need #1 `hqc`). -/
403	private lemma coversTop_preimage_of_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) {I : Type*}
404	    (Z : I → X.Opens) (hZ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).CoversTop Z) :
405	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop (fun i => φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Z i) := by
406	  intro W x hx
407	  obtain ⟨U', f, hf, hU'⟩ := hZ ⊤ (φ.inv.base x) (by trivial)
408	  obtain ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ := hf
409	  have hxZ : φ.inv.base x ∈ Z i := (leOfHom g) hU'
410	  refine ⟨W ⊓ (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Z i), homOfLE inf_le_left,
411	    ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩, ⟨hx, ?_⟩⟩
412	  exact hxZ

438	The genuine wall of `hqc`: a single cross-ring slice structure-sheaf ring map `ψ_r` from which the
439	colimit-preserving `SheafOfModules.pullback ψ_r` is built.  For a scheme iso `φ : X ≅ Y` and an open
440	`Uᵢ ⊆ X` with image `Vᵢ = φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ Uᵢ ⊆ Y`, the underlying opens-equivalence functor is
441	`F₀ = Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens X ⥤ Opens Y` (sending `Uᵢ` to `Vᵢ` by `rfl`).  Its slice version
442	`Over.post F₀ : Over Uᵢ ⥤ Over Vᵢ` is continuous and final (both because `F₀` is an equivalence,
443	`φ` being an iso).  The map `ψ_r` is the slice of the structure-sheaf comparison
444	`φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom : 𝒪_X ⟶ (F₀.sheafPushforwardContinuous _ _ _).obj 𝒪_Y`, obtained simply by
445	applying the over-pullback functor `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).overPullback RingCat Uᵢ`; the
446	codomain matches the desired sliced pushforward by the Beck–Chevalley identity
447	`Over.post F₀ ⋙ Over.forget Vᵢ = Over.forget Uᵢ ⋙ F₀` (`rfl`). -/

467	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)) :=

482	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui))).obj

507	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice equivalence for `φ : X ≅ Y` and its continuity
508	
509	The slice equivalence `Over Uᵢ ≌ Over Vᵢ` (`Vᵢ = φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ Uᵢ`) induced by a scheme iso `φ`, with
510	BOTH its forward and inverse slice functors shown continuous for the over-Grothendieck-topologies.
511	These are the site-equivalence inputs (continuity instances) required by
512	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` / `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` to build the
513	two-pushforward slice adjunction (blueprint `lem:pushforward_slice_two_adjunction`, the `hqc` wall).
514	The inverse continuity carries the `Over.post (Opens.map φ.hom.base) ⋙ Over.map (unitIso.inv)`
515	correction; `Over.map` is always continuous (`instIsContinuousOverMapOver`) and the post-factor is
516	cover-preserving via `coverPreserving_opens_map φ.hom.base`. -/
517	section SliceTwoAdj
518	
519	open TopologicalSpace
520	
521	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) (Ui : X.Opens)
522	
523	/-- `Opens.map φ.hom.base` is an equivalence (twin of `opensMapInvBase_isEquivalence`). -/
524	instance opensMapHomBase_isEquivalence : (Opens.map φ.hom.base).IsEquivalence :=
525	  inferInstanceAs (Opens.mapMapIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso φ)).functor.IsEquivalence
526	
527	/-- The opens-equivalence `Opens X ≌ Opens Y` induced by the scheme iso `φ`.  Built from
528	`mapMapIso` so that its `functor` is (defeq) `Opens.map φ.inv.base` and its `inverse` is (defeq)
529	`Opens.map φ.hom.base` — both honest `Opens.map`s, with known continuity. -/
530	noncomputable def opensEquivOfIso : TopologicalSpace.Opens X ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens Y :=
531	  Opens.mapMapIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso φ).symm
532	
533	/-- The slice equivalence `Over Uᵢ ≌ Over Vᵢ` (`Vᵢ = φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ Uᵢ`). -/
534	noncomputable def sliceOversEquiv :
535	    CategoryTheory.Over Ui ≌ CategoryTheory.Over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui) :=
536	  CategoryTheory.Over.postEquiv Ui (opensEquivOfIso φ)
537	
538	/-- Continuity of the forward slice functor (it is `Over.post (Opens.map φ.inv.base)`). -/
539	instance sliceOversEquiv_functor_isContinuous :
540	    (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).functor.IsContinuous
541	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui)
542	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)) :=
543	  overPost_slice_isContinuous φ Ui
544	
545	/-- Continuity of the post-functor along the opens-equivalence inverse `(opensEquivOfIso φ).inverse`
546	(defeq `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), the first factor of the inverse slice functor.  Stated in the
547	`postEquiv`-native syntax (`Vᵢ = (opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Uᵢ`) so the composite below matches
548	the goal coming out of `Over.postEquiv_inverse`. -/
549	instance overPost_slice_inverse_isContinuous :
550	    (Over.post (X := (opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui) (opensEquivOfIso φ).inverse).IsContinuous
551	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over ((opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui))
552	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over
553	        ((opensEquivOfIso φ).inverse.obj ((opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui))) :=
554	  Functor.isContinuous_of_coverPreserving
555	    (compatiblePreservingOfFlat _ _)
556	    (CoverPreserving.overPost ((opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui)
557	      (coverPreserving_opens_map φ.hom.base))
558	
559	/-- Continuity of the inverse slice functor `eqv.inverse = Over.post (Opens.map φ.hom.base) ⋙
560	Over.map (unitIso.inv)`, the correction-carrying composite. -/
561	instance sliceOversEquiv_inverse_isContinuous :
562	    (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).inverse.IsContinuous
563	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui))
564	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui) := by
565	  have key : ((Over.post (X := (opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui) (opensEquivOfIso φ).inverse).comp
566	      (Over.map ((opensEquivOfIso φ).unitIso.inv.app Ui))).IsContinuous
567	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over ((opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui))
568	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui) :=
569	    @Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ _ _ _
570	      (Over.post (X := (opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui) (opensEquivOfIso φ).inverse)
571	      (Over.map ((opensEquivOfIso φ).unitIso.inv.app Ui))
572	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over ((opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui))
573	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over
574	        ((opensEquivOfIso φ).inverse.obj ((opensEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj Ui)))
575	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui)
576	      (overPost_slice_inverse_isContinuous φ Ui)
577	      (GrothendieckTopology.instIsContinuousOverMapOver (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
578	        ((opensEquivOfIso φ).unitIso.inv.app Ui))
579	  rw [sliceOversEquiv, Over.postEquiv_inverse]
580	  exact key
581	
582	/-- **The reverse slice ring map `φ''`** (blueprint `lem:slice_reverse_ring_map`).  The cross-ring
583	structure-sheaf comparison along the *corrected* inverse slice functor
584	`(sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).inverse`.  Object-level it is the slice restriction of
585	`φ.hom.toRingCatSheafHom`; the `Over.map (unitIso.inv)` correction affects only the morphism action
586	(absorbed by `H₁`/`H₂`).  Built as the over-pullback of `φ.hom.toRingCatSheafHom` transported along
587	the codomain iso induced by `unitIso.inv : φ.hom⁻¹ᵁ Vᵢ ≅ Uᵢ`. -/
588	noncomputable def sliceReverseRingMap :
589	    (Y.ringCatSheaf.over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)) ⟶
590	      ((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
591	        ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui))
592	        ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui)).obj
593	          (X.ringCatSheaf.over Ui) :=
594	  -- φ'' = the over-pullback of `φ.hom.toRingCatSheafHom` (blueprint-correct), i.e.
595	  -- `sliceStructureSheafHom φ.symm Vᵢ`.  It is stated along the BARE
596	  -- `Over.post (Opens.map φ.symm.inv.base)` in `Over (φ.symm.inv⁻¹ᵁ Vᵢ)`, but its codomain is
597	  -- *definitionally equal* to the codomain along the correction-carrying inverse slice functor
598	  -- `eqv.inverse = Over.post (Opens.map φ.symm.inv.base) ⋙ Over.map (unitIso.inv.app Uᵢ)`:
599	  -- both `sheafPushforwardContinuousComp` (composition of pushforward-continuous functors is
600	  -- `Iso.refl`) and `Over.mapForget` (`map g ⋙ forget = forget`, `rfl`) are definitional, so the
601	  -- "codomain bridge" of the blueprint reduces to the identity and is absorbed here by defeq.
602	  sliceStructureSheafHom φ.symm (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)
603	
604	/-- **Counit compatibility square `H₁`** for the slice adjunction (blueprint
605	`lem:pushforward_slice_adjunction_h1`).  The reverse ring map `φ''` and the forward slice ring map
606	`ψ_r` satisfy the counit-naturality square required by `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`, absorbing the
607	`Over.map (unitIso.inv)` correction of the inverse functor (reduces to a proof-irrelevant
608	equality-transport identity along `φ.hom⁻¹ᵁ (φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ Uᵢ) = Uᵢ`). -/
609	lemma pushforwardSliceAdjunctionH1 :
610	    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).symm.toAdjunction.counit)
611	        (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf Ui).obj =
612	      (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui).hom ≫
613	        (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).symm.inverse.op.whiskerLeft (sliceReverseRingMap φ Ui).hom := by
614	  ext U x
615	  simp only [sliceReverseRingMap, sliceStructureSheafHom]
616	  simp [Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom]
617	  -- The two structure-sheaf comparisons `φ.inv.c` and `φ.hom.c` compose to the restriction of
618	  -- `𝒪_X` along the open identity `φ.hom⁻¹ᵁ φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ W = W` (`comp_app` + `congr_app φ.hom_inv_id`).
619	  have key : φ.inv.c.app (op ((unop U).left)) ≫
620	      φ.hom.c.app (op (((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).functor.obj (unop U)).left))
621	      = X.sheaf.obj.map (Over.Hom.left ((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).unitInv.app (unop U))).op := by
622	    show φ.inv.app ((unop U).left) ≫ φ.hom.app _ = _
623	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.Hom.congr_app φ.hom_inv_id]
624	    simp only [Scheme.Hom.id_app]
625	    congr 1
626	  have key2 : (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (φ.inv.c.app (op ((unop U).left))) ≫
627	      (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map
628	        (φ.hom.c.app (op (((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).functor.obj (unop U)).left)))
629	      = (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map
630	        (X.sheaf.obj.map (Over.Hom.left ((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).unitInv.app (unop U))).op) := by
631	    rw [← (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map_comp]
632	    exact congrArg _ key
633	  rw [← RingCat.hom_comp]
634	  erw [key2]
635	  rfl
636	
637	/-- **Unit compatibility square `H₂`** for the slice adjunction (blueprint
638	`lem:pushforward_slice_adjunction_h2`).  Identical in shape to `H₁`: the unit triangle reduces to a
639	proof-irrelevant equality-transport identity along the same open identity. -/
640	lemma pushforwardSliceAdjunctionH2 :
641	    (sliceReverseRingMap φ Ui).hom ≫
642	        (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).symm.functor.op.whiskerLeft (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui).hom ≫
643	          Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).symm.toAdjunction.unit)
644	            (Sheaf.over Y.ringCatSheaf (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)).obj =
645	      𝟙 (Sheaf.over Y.ringCatSheaf (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)).obj := by
646	  ext U x
647	  simp only [sliceReverseRingMap, sliceStructureSheafHom]
648	  simp [Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom]
649	  have key : φ.hom.c.app (op ((unop U).left)) ≫
650	      φ.inv.c.app (op (((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).inverse.obj (unop U)).left)) ≫
651	        Y.sheaf.obj.map (Over.Hom.left ((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).counitInv.app (unop U))).op
652	      = 𝟙 (Y.sheaf.obj.obj (op ((unop U).left))) := by
653	    rw [← Category.assoc,
654	      show φ.hom.c.app (op ((unop U).left)) ≫
655	          φ.inv.c.app (op (((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).inverse.obj (unop U)).left))
656	          = (φ.inv ≫ φ.hom).app ((unop U).left) from (Scheme.Hom.comp_app φ.inv φ.hom _).symm,
657	      Scheme.Hom.congr_app φ.inv_hom_id]
658	    erw [Category.id_comp, ← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
659	    exact (congrArg Y.presheaf.map (Subsingleton.elim _ _)).trans
660	      (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id Y.presheaf _)
661	  have key2 : (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (φ.hom.c.app (op ((unop U).left))) ≫
662	      (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map
663	        (φ.inv.c.app (op (((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).inverse.obj (unop U)).left))) ≫
664	      (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map
665	        (Y.sheaf.obj.map (Over.Hom.left ((sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).counitInv.app (unop U))).op)
666	      = 𝟙 _ := by
667	    rw [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, key]
668	    exact (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map_id _
669	  rw [← RingCat.hom_comp, ← RingCat.hom_comp]
670	  erw [key2]
671	  rfl
672	
673	/-- **The two-pushforward slice adjunction** (blueprint `lem:pushforward_slice_two_adjunction`):
674	`pushforward φ'' ⊣ pushforward ψ_r`, assembled by feeding `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` the slice
675	equivalence's adjunction, the two ring maps `φ''`/`ψ_r`, and the compatibility squares `H₁`/`H₂`. -/
676	noncomputable def pushforwardSliceTwoAdjunction :
677	    SheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (sliceReverseRingMap φ Ui) ⊣
678	      SheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui) := by
679	  haveI hF : (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).symm.functor.IsContinuous
680	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui))
681	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui) :=
682	    sliceOversEquiv_inverse_isContinuous φ Ui
683	  haveI hG : (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).symm.inverse.IsContinuous
684	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui)
685	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)) :=
686	    sliceOversEquiv_functor_isContinuous φ Ui
687	  exact SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj
688	    (sliceOversEquiv φ Ui).symm.toAdjunction
689	    (sliceReverseRingMap φ Ui) (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui)
690	    (pushforwardSliceAdjunctionH1 φ Ui) (pushforwardSliceAdjunctionH2 φ Ui)
691	
692	/-- **The slice pullback comparison iso** (blueprint `lem:pushforward_slice_pullback_iso`).  Applying
693	the pullback `pullback ψ_r` to the slice `H.over Uᵢ` is isomorphic to the restricted pushforward
694	`(Φ H).over Vᵢ`.  Step 1: `pullback ψ_r ≅ pushforward φ''` by uniqueness of left adjoints
695	(`leftAdjointUniq` of `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction ψ_r` against `pushforwardSliceTwoAdjunction`).
696	Step 2: the rfl-clean section identity `(pushforward φ'').obj (H.over Uᵢ) ≅ (Φ H).over Vᵢ`
697	(both sides have sections `Γ(H, φ.hom⁻¹ᵁ W.left)` by `pushforward_obj_obj`). -/
698	noncomputable def pushforwardSlicePullbackIso (H : X.Modules) :
699	    (SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui)).obj (H.over Ui) ≅
700	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui) :=
701	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq
702	    (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui))
703	    (pushforwardSliceTwoAdjunction φ Ui)).app (H.over Ui) ≪≫ Iso.refl _
704	
705	set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in
706	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
707	-- Heartbeat bumps: the per-slice presentation transport triggers synthesis of the sliced/doubly
708	-- sliced `HasSheafify`/`WEqualsLocallyBijective`/`HasSheafCompose` instances over `(gt Y).over Vᵢ`,
709	-- whose default-heartbeat search times out (it succeeds when bumped).
710	/-- **Pushforward along a scheme iso preserves quasi-coherence** (blueprint
711	`lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`; Need #1 `hqc`).  Quasi-coherence is local: move each local
712	presentation of `H` (over the cover member `Uᵢ`) across the colimit-preserving pullback
713	`pullback ψ_r`, then across the comparison iso `pushforwardSlicePullbackIso` to a presentation of
714	`(Φ H).over Vᵢ`; conclude per-slice quasi-coherence and glue via
715	`pushforward_iso_qcoh_of_slice_qcoh`. -/
716	lemma pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) (H : X.Modules)
717	    (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) :
718	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := by
719	  obtain ⟨qcd⟩ := hH.nonempty_quasicoherentData
720	  refine pushforward_iso_qcoh_of_slice_qcoh φ H qcd (fun i => ?_)
721	  -- Per-slice: build a presentation of `(Φ H).over (φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ qcd.X i)`.
722	  haveI hFinal : (sliceOversEquiv φ (qcd.X i)).functor.Final := inferInstance
723	  -- the unit comparison `η : (pullback ψ_r).obj (unit) ≅ unit` (iso since the slice functor is Final).
724	  have η : (SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ (qcd.X i))).obj
725	        (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over (qcd.X i))) ≅
726	      SheafOfModules.unit (Y.ringCatSheaf.over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ qcd.X i)) :=
727	    asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ (qcd.X i)))
728	  -- transport the local presentation of `H.over Uᵢ` across the colimit-preserving pullback...
729	  have P1 : ((SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ (qcd.X i))).obj
730	      (H.over (qcd.X i))).Presentation :=
731	    (qcd.presentation i).map (SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ (qcd.X i))) η
732	  -- ...then across the comparison iso to a presentation of `(Φ H).over Vᵢ`, whence quasi-coherence.
733	  exact (P1.ofIsIso (pushforwardSlicePullbackIso φ (qcd.X i) H).hom).isQuasicoherent
734

737	/- Planner strategy:
738	Part (1): `R^q j_* H` is the sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)` (presheaf description
739	`higher_direct_image_presheaf`, Stacks 01XJ); for affine `V`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine (U affine,
740	X separated), so `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `H^q` for `q ≥ 1`; affine opens are a basis ⇒
741	`R^q j_* H = 0`. Part (2): take an injective resolution `H → I•`; `j_* I•` is a resolution of
742	`j_* H` (each `j_* Iⁿ` is `j_*`-acyclic by (1)) and each `j_* Iⁿ` is `f_*`-acyclic (same presheaf
743	+ Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); so `j_* I•` is an `f_*`-acyclic resolution of `j_* H`,
744	and the P4 acyclic-resolution comparison (`acyclic_resolution_computes_derived` /
745	`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) with `G = f_*` gives `R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ H^k(f_*(j_* I•)) =
746	H^k((f∘j)_* I•) = R^k (f∘j)_* H`, using `f_* ∘ j_* = (f∘j)_*`. Blueprint:
747	`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`. Source: Stacks `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`.

750	/-- **Higher direct images of an affine open immersion vanish** (part (1) of blueprint
751	`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`; Stacks `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
752	
753	For an open immersion `j : U ↪ X` where `U` is affine and `X` is separated, the morphism
754	`j` is affine (since for any affine open `V ⊆ X`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine by separatedness).
755	Therefore `R^q j_* H = 0` for every `q ≥ 1` and every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H`.
756	
757	Proof route: by `higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology`, `R^q j_* H` is the
758	sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)`. For affine `V`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine (`U` affine
759	+ `X` separated), and `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `H^q` for `q ≥ 1`. Since affine opens
760	form a basis, the presheaf homology is locally zero, and the site lemmas
761	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective` (in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`,
762	importable via `AffineSerreVanishing`) collapse the sheafification to zero. -/
763	private lemma isAffineHom_of_affine_separated (j : U ⟶ X) [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated] :
764	    IsAffineHom j := by
765	  have hg : IsSeparated (terminal.from X) := Scheme.IsSeparated.isSeparated_terminal_from
766	  have hcomp : IsAffineHom (j ≫ terminal.from X) := by
767	    have he : j ≫ terminal.from X = terminal.from U := terminal.hom_ext _ _
768	    rw [he]; infer_instance
769	  exact IsAffineHom.of_comp j (terminal.from X)

771	/-- The sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor on `U`-modules, `M ↦ Γ(W, j_* M) = Γ(j⁻¹W, M)`,
772	realised as the composite `pushforward j ⋙ forget ⋙ restrictScalars 𝟙 ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ eval(W)`.
773	Its `q`-th right derived functor applied to `H` is the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
774	Project-local: a named composite so its additivity instance is synthesised once at the top
775	level (the 5-fold composite defeats in-proof instance search). -/
776	noncomputable def pushforwardSectionsFunctor (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
777	    U.Modules ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
778	  Scheme.Modules.pushforward j ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
779	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
780	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
781	    (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)

783	noncomputable instance pushforwardSectionsFunctor_additive
784	    (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).Additive := by
785	  unfold pushforwardSectionsFunctor

788	  haveI hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j).Additive := inferInstance
789	  haveI i4 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
790	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
791	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
792	  haveI i3 : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
793	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
794	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
795	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Acyclic resolutions compute right-derived functors
10	
11	This file provides the abstract homological-algebra core underlying the Čech
12	computation of higher direct images (Stacks Tag 015E, Leray's acyclicity lemma):
13	**an acyclic resolution computes every right-derived functor**.
14	
15	Throughout, `𝒜` and `ℬ` are abelian categories, `𝒜` has injective resolutions
16	(so that `G.rightDerived n` is everywhere defined), and `G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ` is an
17	additive functor.
18	
19	## Declarations
20	
21	* `CategoryTheory.Functor.IsRightAcyclic` — typeclass for right-`G`-acyclic objects.
22	* Instance: every injective object is right-acyclic (from Mathlib's
23	  `Functor.isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ`).
24	
25	The following declarations are all constructed and proved axiom-clean in this file
26	(P4 complete — see the marker at the end of the file):
27
